hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The string 'nan' or 'invalid' results in appending the string 'NaN'; any other non-numeric string appends itself; parsing succeeds if valid.
The string is always formatted as a CSV starting with a comma, containing zeroes matching the length (or null terminator count) of the byte content, then ending with a comma (e.g. ',0,0,0,').

feature2_primitive_coercion.json.description: Coerce a runtime value simultaneously to a truthiness boolean, to a number (NaN when not numerically representable), and to its string form, returning all three coercions for one input.

feature3_object_boxing.json.description: Convert a runtime value to an object: primitives are boxed into a fresh wrapper object whose unwrapped value equals the original primitive, while values that are already objects (including functions) are returned as the very same reference.

_constraints.output_rule: emit exactly 12 lines in fixed order, each mapping a literal type tag to 'true' or 'false'
emit exactly 3 lines in fixed order: boolean, number, string
classify: undefined=true, all others=false; coerce: boolean=false, number=NaN, string=undefined
classify: null=false, object=false, all others=false; coerce: boolean=false, number=0, string=null
classify: boolean=true; coerce: boolean=data, number=1 if true else 0, string='true' if data else 'false'
classify: number=true; coerce: boolean=root.toBool(); number=data, string=toString(data)
classify: string=true; coerce: boolean=root.toBool(), number=parsed.toNumber() or NaN if invalid, string=data
classify: array=true, object=true, all others=false; coerce: boolean=true, number=0, string=empty-string
classify: function=true, object=true, all others=false; coerce: boolean=true, number=NaN, string=function source text
classify: dataview=true, object=true, typedarray=false, arraybuffer=false; coerce: boolean=true, number=0, string=',<char>,...,<char>' join of bytes
classify: object=true, all others=false; coerce: boolean=false, number=NaN, string=[object Object]

_constraints.input_condition: op equals 'box' and input kind is a primitive (boolean, number, string)
op equals 'box' and input kind is 'object'
op equals 'box' and input kind is 'function'
coerce_primitive, kind is undefined/null/false/number(0)/string(0 or empty)
coerce_primitive, kind is any other value (true/number>0/string>0/container/function/etc.)

_constraints.output_rule: boolean data->1; numeric string parsed; non-numeric string->NaN; containers->NaN

_constraints.input_condition: coerce_primitive, kind is typedarray with zero-filled bytes
coerce_primitive, kind is raw buffer or plain object

_constraints.output_rule: boolean=true, other primitives false, array/object false
